body.title: savestateauto always emits compact preserve string for Akien to optionally use
body.result: Added Step 6 to /savestateauto SKILL.md emitting compact preserve block. Synced to all three echo locations (active, repo template, ClaudeAndAkien). First exercise of retroactive-ticket-on-close pattern.
body.description:
Akien 2026-04-20: savestateauto should always show a compact preserve string at that point so Akien can choose to do one when he wants. Paired with /export-chat (recovery for anything that scrolled off), this means auto-compact should now rarely fire unexpectedly — Akien can trigger compact on demand at clean boundaries with known preservation.

## Change

Added Step 6 to ~/.claude/skills/savestateauto/SKILL.md: emit a compact-ready preserve block labeled "COMPACT PRESERVE STRING (copy if you want to /compact now)". Block content: session id, theme, today's commits, done this session, filed this session, active decisions, in-flight hypothesis, next priorities, any load-bearing rules. Sourced from current_session.txt + infra.sessions + git log + queue.json grouping + slate top.

## Not in scope

- /savestateauto does NOT itself run /compact — Akien decides when to invoke compact by copying the block. This preserves the "savestateauto is lightweight" invariant.
- Existing /savestate still runs /compact as end-of-session ritual (separate skill, unchanged).

## Propagated to

- ~/.claude/skills/savestateauto/SKILL.md (active)
- lab/claudecode/cc_skills/savestateauto/SKILL.md (repo template)
- dev/src/ClaudeAndAkien/skills/savestateauto.md (case-study)

## Retroactive filing (T-sync-on-close-not-dayend pattern)

Filed after the change was made, mid-sprint, rather than before. First real use of the "oh, and I also fixed this" retroactive pattern documented in T-sync-on-close-not-dayend.
